cards.back: A synchronization mechanism where multiple processes compete for access to a shared resource, ensuring that only one process can use the resource at a time.
The process of managing interactions among multiple processes in a distributed system to achieve a common goal.
A mutually acceptable decision made by multiple processes in a distributed system, leading to a shared understanding or action.
A synchronization mechanism where a shared token is passed from one process to another, allowing only one process to access a shared resource at a time.
Two properties of distributed systems that affect the performance and reliability of message passing between processes.
